name: Anika Rao
degrees: B.Tech in Computer Science, Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Hyderabad, 2022
MSc in Cognitive Neuroscience, University of Edinburgh, 2024
target_countries_freeform: United States, United Kingdom, and Australia
stated_interests: Neuroimaging biomarkers of PTSD and trauma using fMRI and DWI
Computational psychiatry: reinforcement-learning models of decision-making in mood and anxiety disorders
Affective computing: multimodal detection of emotional state from speech and physiology
resume_text: Anika Rao. MSc Cognitive Neuroscience (Edinburgh, distinction); B.Tech CS (IIT Hyderabad). Research assistant in the Edinburgh Clinical Brain Sciences lab (2023-2024): preprocessed 3T fMRI data for a PTSD cohort, built nipype pipelines, fit hierarchical Bayesian RL models in PyMC. Summer intern, affective-computing startup (2021): trained CNN-LSTM models on RAVDESS speech-emotion data and wearable PPG signals. Skills: Python, PyTorch, FSL, FreeSurfer, nilearn, hierarchical Bayesian modeling, signal processing. Languages: English, Hindi, Telugu.
intro_call_summary: Anika wants a PhD bridging clinical neuroscience and machine learning. Strongest pull is toward computational models of trauma and anxiety, ideally combining task-based fMRI with RL/Bayesian modeling. Open to a more applied affective-computing lab as a backup. Needs full funding; will require a student visa. Prefers groups that actively publish clinical-population work, not purely methodological imaging groups. Wants supervisors who take international students.
notable_outputs: Rao, A. et al. (2024). 'Hierarchical Bayesian reinforcement-learning accounts of avoidance in trauma-exposed adults.' MSc thesis, University of Edinburgh.
Co-authored poster, OHBM 2024: 'Amygdala-prefrontal connectivity and PTSD symptom severity in a 3T resting-state cohort.'
Open-source: 'rl-avoidance', a PyMC package for fitting avoidance-learning models (GitHub, 40 stars).
